Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible early renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), raising suspicion for renal tubular dysfunction or possible urinary tract pathology. HbA1c is low, which may reflect anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. No evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical findings.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
